_comment: Parameterized config for the Verified Monitoring (N1) synthetic survey. dashboard_state.py generates per-round, row-level household records (each round = its own opp; a 'primary' research form + a 'back_check' form) and computes every dashboard KPI from those rows via commcare_connect.labs.synthetic.generator.core.survey_quality. Numbers below are GENERATION TARGETS — the displayed KPIs are computed from the generated records, so they land near these with sampling noise. Tune this file to recreate the dashboard for a different program/geography.
_study_comment: Read by the microplans study-design CREATION seeder (scripts/walkthroughs/study-design/ensure_study.py). One round = one two-arm STUDY GROUP of per-ward microplans on the labs-only program 10008 (= opportunity_id; labs-only program id is the positive backing opp id, >= 10000 floor). treatment->intervention arm, comparison->comparison arm; both arms sampled with the SHARED `sampling` config below so they're comparable by construction. 30 PSUs x 10 primary ~= 300 (the canonical best-practice defaults shared with the plan-creation UI / FrameConfig), matching the ~300 households/round the monitoring sim (arms.treatment.n_per_round) surveys. `live_demo_round` (1-indexed) is the round the creation walkthrough re-creates live on camera (reset first); the other rounds are the always-present backdrop both narratives share.

study._sampling_comment: Left EMPTY on purpose: the study inherits the canonical sampling defaults from commcare_connect/microplans/sampling/defaults.py (30 PSUs / 10 primary / 5 alternate / 3 size-balance bands) via FrameConfig.from_payload — the SAME defaults the plan-creation UI uses, so the synthetic plans and the UI draw identically. Add keys to `sampling` only to deliberately diverge from the canonical defaults.

_geography_comment: ROTATING wards: the program works a DIFFERENT ward each bi-monthly cycle, verified against an adjacent comparison ward. All 12 wards are REAL, adjacent pairs in the Kaura LGA area (Kaduna State, Nigeria), sourced from the labs admin-boundary backend (geo/kaura_lga_wards.geojson). Survey + service-delivery points are sampled INSIDE these real polygons; the map moves to each round's pair.
_map_pin_cap_comment: Max survey pins DRAWN per ward (per-ward random subsample for legibility — not a data limit). Set above the full per-ward sample (30 PSUs x 10 primary = ~300) so every PSU shows ALL its surveys when you zoom in to inspect, instead of a thinned ~5/PSU.
_flagged_surveyor_comment: One deliberately-degraded program-ward surveyor (T6): lower evidence-photo, and — the catch — their originals agree LESS with the independent re-survey (back-check) and carry the Layer-3 fabrication signature (curbstoned duration + collapsed roof-mix). GPS-within-15m is NOT a T6 signal: the app gates submission at 15 m, so T6's accepted captures are within the gate like everyone's — the GPS discrepancy surfaces only in the back-check (Type-2). Makes the per-surveyor scorecard surface a real outlier without an unrealistic GPS-gate violation.

flagged_surveyor._signature_comment: Layer-3 fabrication signature the Distributions screen catches with no re-visit: curbstoned ~9-min interviews (duration_mean/sd) and roof-type answers collapsed onto the modal value (roof_concentration share). Honest surveyors get natural between-area variation via surveyor_heterogeneity, so the screen reads as discriminating, not rigged.

_distributions_comment: Honest surveyors vary their answer-mix and pace by working area (surveyor_heterogeneity, kept mild so honest roof HHI ~0.30-0.35 and pace ~16-20 — wide enough for a believable z but never rivalling the flagged surveyor); the Layer-3 screen profiles the roof_type answer distribution (uniformity_field) with a robust MAD z, flagging at z_threshold.
_primary_rate_comment: Share of completed surveys that land on the PRIMARY (first-choice) sampled household vs a ranked ALTERNATE backup, when the round is grounded on a live plan. Drawn per surveyor ~ Normal(mean, variance); the flagged surveyor (T6) substitutes far more (flagged_mean), so heavy substitution is part of why they're flagged. A 'good run' = high primary rate.
_self_report_comment: Program self-report = verified coverage * inflation (the program over-reports), interpolated start->end across rounds, with +/- noise so the self-reported line jitters round-to-round and sits above the verified line. R6: ~0.681*1.29 -> ~88%.

quality._gps_gate_comment: The CommCare app gates form submission at <=15 m from the assigned household, so EVERY accepted capture is within the gate: gps_within_15m=1.0 and the offset is the near band only (realistic GPS noise that hugs the building footprint). The GPS fraud signal therefore lives in the back-check (Type-2: the re-survey lands far from the fabricator's claimed household), not the primary scorecard.

_backcheck_comment: A stratified sample (every enumerator covered) is re-surveyed by a different (BC) enumerator. outcome_agreement drives the vitamin-A match rate (the 'back-check pass' headline); type1_agreement drives discordance on stable identifiers (sex/age/present). Front-loaded to 20% in the first rounds per J-PAL/DIME guidance.
